vocab.meaning: một tờ giấy trắng/khởi đầu mới chưa có sự tác động
vocab.example: Back then, my mind was like a blank canvas, ready to be filled with all the colors and techniques my teacher taught me.
I used to spend hours trying to make my sketches picture-perfect, though they usually ended up being quite messy.

vocab.meaning: nắm vững các kỹ năng cơ bản
vocab.example: It took me a while to master the ropes of perspective drawing when I was in primary school.

vocab.meaning: học thử/làm thử một cách không quá chuyên sâu
vocab.example: I used to dabble in oil painting during the summer holidays, but I never pursued it seriously.

vocab.meaning: một tia sáng sáng tạo/nguồn cảm hứng
vocab.example: My art teacher really helped ignite a creative spark in me that lasted throughout my childhood.

vocab.meaning: nhìn nhận cái gì đó một cách đúng đắn/về mặt phối cảnh
vocab.example: Learning to draw landscapes really helped me put the scale of the natural world into perspective.
As a kid, my paintings were often a bit off the wall, featuring purple trees and orange skies.

vocab.meaning: mô tả/làm gì đó một cách khái quát, không đi vào chi tiết
vocab.example: When I first started, I tended to paint with a broad brush because I lacked the patience for intricate details.

vocab.meaning: đến mức hoàn hảo/cực kỳ chi tiết
vocab.example: I remember once trying to draw a dragon decorated to the nines with golden scales and emerald eyes.

vocab.meaning: đắm chìm/say sưa vào việc gì
vocab.example: I would often lose myself in my sketchbook for hours, completely oblivious to everything happening around me.
